profile.title: Add bail-on-test-failure handling to Testem
profile.summary: Add configurable early bailout on test failures across runners, reporters, and exit codes.
profile.description: Add configurable early bailout on test failures across runners, reporters, and exit codes. Add `bail_on_test_failure` with false, true, or positive-integer thresholds and validate invalid values. Stop on the configured non-skipped failure count and expose resettable reason, launcher, count, and failed-test state. Emit bailout summaries through TAP, Dot, TeamCity, and XUnit reporters. Make runner, server, and application abort operations idempotent and suppress later results or errors. Guard Mocha, Jasmine2, and QUnit browser emissions after abort, including deferred callbacks. Return a distinct bail exit error using only the reported bail reason and pre-bail test count.
difficulty_factors: changes span multiple components or interfaces

extraction.method: frontier LLM semantic extraction with pinned-source review

profile.title: Validate daemon watch, status, and log lifecycle
profile.summary: Add daemon watch validation, state tracking, logging, and lifecycle commands with non-blocking run-once processing.
profile.description: Add daemon watch validation, state tracking, logging, and lifecycle commands with non-blocking run-once processing. Add daemon lifecycle, run-once, validation, state, watch, batch, logging, and notification CLI options. Scan only top-level files, avoid network and prompts, and move stable files without overwriting destinations. Combine CLI and JSON-configured watch paths while validating required path, movie directory, and exclusion fields. Create non-empty state before daemon processing and append per-cycle logs at the state-derived log path. Apply stability checks and a global batch-size cap while skipping only `.part` suffixes. Use exit code 2 for invalid lifecycle or configuration requests and keep dry-run free of state, log, and file changes.
difficulty_factors: changes span multiple components or interfaces

extraction.method: frontier LLM semantic extraction with pinned-source review

profile.title: Add automatic table of contents generation for Obsidian linter
profile.summary: Implement an opt-in rule that generates and updates a TOC from document headings.
profile.description: Implement an opt-in rule that generates and updates a TOC from document headings. Implement a new rule, export default `AutoToc` from `src/rules/auto-toc.ts`, that generates or updates a TOC. Opt-in via `<!-- toc -->`. If absent, return input unchanged. The TOC region uses `<!-- toc -->` and `<!-- /toc -->` (case-insensitive, whitespace-tolerant). Ensure blank lines after the start marker, after an optional `title` line, before the end marker, and after the end marker. Include only ATX headings (`#`), filtered by `minLevel`/`maxLevel`.
difficulty_factors: strict behavioral compatibility or exact-output validation
changes span multiple components or interfaces

profile.title: Add single-active-consumer priority and cancel tracking to virtual transports
profile.summary: Add single-active-consumer semantics, consumer priority selection, cancel notifications, and lifecycle tracking to virtual transports.
profile.description: Add single-active-consumer semantics, consumer priority selection, cancel notifications, and lifecycle tracking to virtual transports. When a queue is declared with `x-single-active-consumer: True` in its queue arguments, at most one consumer receives messages at a time. `Channel.basic_consume` supports consumer priority via `x-priority` in consumer arguments (default 0) and an optional `on_cancel` callback. Consumer state must live in `BrokerState` (shared across channels), not per-channel. The `connection._callbacks[queue]` entry must dispatch to the correct consumer at delivery time, not simply store the last registered callback. When a higher-priority consumer registers on a SAC queue where a lower-priority consumer is active, the lower-priority consumer is demoted and its `on_cancel` fires. Transports with class-level `global_state` (memory, filesystem, pyro) must clear consumer state when a new Transport is created, since registrations must not leak across connections.
difficulty_factors: quantitative performance, accuracy, or resource constraints

extraction.method: frontier LLM semantic extraction with pinned-source review

profile.title: Add task graph export with JSON, DOT, and text output
profile.summary: Add a graph export command for tasks with JSON, DOT, and text output, including reverse traversal and status control.
profile.description: Add a graph export command for tasks with JSON, DOT, and text output, including reverse traversal and status control. Add a `--graph` view with JSON, DOT, and indented text formats. Include roots, node metadata, dependency and command edges, depth groups, and longest path in JSON output. Mark up-to-date DOT nodes as dashed and suppress status fields or styling under `no-status`. Support reverse dependency traversal across the complete Taskfile. Resolve aliases, wildcards, includes, defaults, and for-loop expansions consistently. Report missing tasks and dependency cycles with errors that name the affected tasks.
difficulty_factors: quantitative performance, accuracy, or resource constraints
changes span multiple components or interfaces
